description: Master modern web development frameworks and technologies

quizzes.title: GraphQL APIs
quizzes.description: Master GraphQL for efficient API queries, mutations, and real-time subscriptions.

questions.question: What is GraphQL?

options: Clients can request exactly the data they need

questions.question: What are the three main operations in GraphQL?

options: Defines the types and relationships in your API

options: A function that returns data for a field

questions.question: Which tool is commonly used as a GraphQL client?

options: A reusable piece of query logic

questions.question: What are subscriptions in GraphQL?
What is Apollo Server?

options: Ability to query the schema itself

questions.question: What is the purpose of variables in GraphQL queries?

options: Combining multiple queries into one request

questions.question: What is GraphiQL?
options: An in-browser IDE for exploring GraphQL

questions.question: What is the purpose of directives in GraphQL?
options: To conditionally include or skip fields

options: Multiple database queries for nested data

questions.question: What are some solutions to N+1 problem?

options: A type that can represent multiple object types

questions.question: What is an interface in GraphQL?
options: Abstract type that other types can implement

questions.question: What is the purpose of @deprecated directive?
What is query complexity analysis?

options: Storing queries on server for security/performance

questions.question: Can GraphQL work with REST APIs?
options: Yes, it can wrap existing REST APIs

questions.question: What is the purpose of context in GraphQL?

options: Combining multiple GraphQL schemas into one

questions.question: What is the difference between query and mutation?
options: Query is for reading, mutation for writing

quizzes.title: Web Performance & Optimization
quizzes.description: Learn performance metrics, lazy loading, caching, and optimization techniques.

questions.question: What is Core Web Vitals?
options: Google's metrics for web page user experience

questions.question: Which metric measures load time of the largest content element?
What is the recommended LCP threshold for good user experience?
What does CLS measure?
What is First Input Delay (FID)?
options: Time from first user interaction to browser response

questions.question: What is tree shaking?
What is code splitting?
options: Splitting code into smaller bundles loaded on demand

questions.question: What is lazy loading?
What is the purpose of CDN?
options: To serve static assets from servers closer to users

questions.question: What is browser caching?
options: Storing resources locally to avoid re-downloading

questions.question: What are critical rendering paths?
options: Steps browser takes to render a page

questions.question: What is minification?
What is gzip compression?
What is the purpose of prefetching?
options: Loading resources before they are needed

questions.question: What is Time to First Byte (TTFB)?
options: Time between request and first byte of response

questions.question: What is server-side rendering (SSR)?
options: Rendering HTML on server for faster initial load

questions.question: What is the benefit of using WebP images?
What is HTTP/2 advantage over HTTP/1.1?
What is the purpose of a sitemap?
What is the 'above the fold' concept?
What is a render-blocking resource?
options: Resource that prevents page rendering until loaded

questions.question: How to defer JavaScript loading?
What is the benefit of using SVG images?
What is the purpose of a service worker?
What is the 'critical CSS' technique?
options: Inlining CSS needed for above-the-fold content

questions.question: What is the benefit of using font-display: swap?
options: Prevents invisible text while fonts load

questions.question: What is the purpose of resource hints like preconnect?
What is the 'waterfall' in browser dev tools?
What is the benefit of reducing DOM size?

options: Setting limits for file sizes and metrics
